nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Itatiaia spinetail
nodes.summary: Itatiaia spinetail（Itatiaia spinetail，学名 Asthenes moreirae），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括气候变化。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 米纳斯吉拉斯州
nodes.summary: 米纳斯吉拉斯州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联94种鸟类，包括小拟䳍、孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍等94种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 圣埃斯皮里图州
nodes.summary: 圣埃斯皮里图州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联59种鸟类，包括孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍、East Brazilian chachalaca等59种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 圣保罗州
nodes.summary: 圣保罗州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联55种鸟类，包括孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Russet-crowned crake等55种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 里約熱內盧州
nodes.summary: 里約熱內盧州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联39种鸟类，包括孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Rufous-vented ground cuckoo等39种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: Serra dos Órgãos
nodes.summary: Serra dos Órgãos是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Itatiaia spinetail，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Itatiaia spinetail is found in the mountains of southeastern Brazil's Minas Gerais, São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and Espírito Santo states

links.evidence: The Itatiaia spinetail is found in the mountains of southeastern Brazil's Minas Gerais, São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and Espírito Santo states

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Itatiaia spinetail is found in the mountains of southeastern Brazil's Minas Gerais, São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and Espírito Santo states

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Itatiaia spinetail is found in the mountains of southeastern Brazil's Minas Gerais, São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and Espírito Santo states

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is named for Serra do Itatiaia and also occurs in Serra do Caraça and Serra dos Órgãos

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It inhabits semi-humid to humid montane scrublands, Chusquea bamboo thickets, and areas of tall grass near scrub

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Stresses to its habitat due to climate change are expected to adversely affect the species. It is considered uncommon to locally fairly common and occurs in two national parks
